title: Minesweeper
description: Clear the minefield without detonating a single bomb. Flag the mines, use your logic, and survive.
controls: Left-click to reveal, right-click to flag
howToPlay:
Left-click any cell to reveal it. If the cell is safe, it shows a number indicating how many of its eight neighbouring cells contain mines. Use those numbers to deduce which cells are safe and which hide mines.

Right-click (or long-press on mobile) a cell to place a flag on it, marking it as a suspected mine. Flag all mines and reveal every safe cell to win. If you click a mine, the game is over.

The first click is always safe — the board is generated after your first move so you can never lose immediately. Work outward from open areas, cross-reference numbers, and use process of elimination to clear the whole board.
about:
Minesweeper was popularised by its inclusion in Microsoft Windows 3.1 in 1992, where it was originally intended to teach users how to use a mouse. It quickly became one of the most-played computer games of all time, with millions of people learning to count mines during coffee breaks throughout the 1990s and 2000s.

The version on kloopik is a faithful recreation of the classic 9x9 beginner layout with 10 mines. It runs entirely in your browser using HTML5 Canvas — no plugins required.
